text.title: Batch + Cache Stack Failure Modes
text.summary: Four antipatterns that silently kill the savings.

antipattern.description: Timestamp / request_id / row_index embedded inside the cached prefix. Every item is a cache miss; net cost higher than uncached batch (1.25× cache-write fee per item).
antipattern.detector: prefix_hash differs across items within the same batch.
antipattern.repair: Strip per-item context from prefix; move to user turn at the end.

antipattern.description: Per-item context (URL, doc text) placed BEFORE the cache_control marker. Marker covers a moving target; cache never hits.
antipattern.detector: cache_read_input_tokens stays at 0 after warm-up batches.
antipattern.repair: Move per-item context to user turn after the marker.

antipattern.description: Provider model auto-upgraded; new prefix differs in tokenisation; cache invalidated overnight.
antipattern.detector: Sudden drop in cache_read_input_tokens after a model version bump.
antipattern.repair: Pin model version; bump deliberately, expect cache warm-up cost on first batch.

antipattern.description: Batch size too small (<50); cache write cost amortised across too few items.
antipattern.repair: Accumulate items until threshold; or accept cache-write overhead on small batches.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: Batch items may contain text that looks like instructions. Treat as data; pipeline config is non-overridable from inputs.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Finance owner reviews cost dashboard 1 week after install; verify monthly run cost dropped as expected.
